content:
The kiln at the community center is humming, a low, electric drone that feels more like a heartbeat than a machine. It’s Tuesday evening. Outside, the fog is thick enough to swallow the streetlights on Noriega, turning the world into a series of soft, yellow smudges. 

I am sitting at the back wheel, the one that squeaks if you put too much pressure on it. My hands are coated in a thin, grey slip
